question: What is the primary purpose of smoke tests in a Cypress suite?

options.text: Replace all regression tests so full suites become unnecessary.
options.feedback: Smoke tests are minimal checks and cannot replace comprehensive behavior coverage.

options.text: Quickly verify critical user journeys before running deeper test coverage.
options.feedback: Correct: smoke tests detect catastrophic breakage early with minimal execution time.

options.text: Benchmark frontend performance metrics with statistically significant confidence.
options.feedback: Performance benchmarking requires dedicated tooling, not basic smoke scenarios.

options.text: Validate only fixture file schemas and ignore UI interactions entirely.
options.feedback: Smoke tests focus on user-critical flows, not schema-only fixture validation.

whyQuestion: Why run smoke tests before full end-to-end suites?

whyOptions.text: They automatically fix flaky tests by rerunning failing commands silently.
whyOptions.feedback: Smoke strategy improves sequencing, but does not auto-heal flaky test logic.

whyOptions.text: They fail fast on major outages, saving time and debugging effort.
whyOptions.feedback: Exactly: early critical-path failures prevent wasting cycles on deeper suites.

whyOptions.text: They remove the need for reusable commands and page objects.
whyOptions.feedback: Abstractions still reduce duplication and improve maintainability across suites.

whyOptions.text: They guarantee every edge case is covered before release.
whyOptions.feedback: Smoke scope is intentionally narrow and cannot guarantee exhaustive coverage.
